$comment: DEFAULT voice profile bundled with the koko-ship skill. This is a generic starting point — any user receiving the skill should override locally with their own .bip-voice.json (project-local) or ~/.bip-voice.json (user-global). The skill loads in priority order: project-local > user-global > this default.

language.rule: Generate in the user's primary language. Default English for global audience.

tone.summary: Direct, lowercase, casual. Decisive but unpretentious. Beats over speeches.
do: lowercase by default (caps only for emphasis, brand names, sentence-internal proper nouns)
short declarative beats — one idea per line
concrete numbers and specific outcomes (dollars, days, count)
underdog framing when honest: solo / no funding / on weekends / from phone
let the build itself be the flex — no hype adjectives
use contractions naturally (kinda, gonna, it's, i've)
dont: no marketing voice ('excited to announce', 'thrilled to share', 'proud to introduce')
no formal capitalization of every sentence
no humble bragging via false modesty
no jargon walls — if it sounds like a press release, rewrite
no questions to fish for engagement ('what do you think?')

specificity_rules.$comment: These rules exist because the #1 failure mode is generic AI output. They must pass before a draft is delivered.

one_moment_rule.description: One post = one moment. Never combine multiple distinct things into one post. If a session has multiple moments, ask the user to pick one (or offer a thread, only if they explicitly want it).
one_moment_rule.rationale: Combined-moment posts read as feature lists. Feature lists score 1-3 on Story.

anti_tagline_rule.description: Forbidden: three or more nouns in parallel structure as a feature list. ('strategist, daily operator, pixel dashboard' is a tagline, not a moment.)
anti_tagline_rule.rationale: Taglines are the marketing voice signature. They auto-fail Authenticity.

would_anyone_else_say_this_test.description: Before delivering, mentally ask: could 100 other indie hackers post this exact text? If yes, the post is generic. Reject and rewrite.
would_anyone_else_say_this_test.rationale: If only the project name is unique, the post has no value.

show_dont_summarize_rule.description: Forbidden: 'built X for Y' or 'launched X' as the only content. Required: at least one specific moment from the actual session — a decision, a struggle, a small detail, an exact thing the user said, an exact thing that broke.
show_dont_summarize_rule.rationale: Summaries are project descriptions. Stories are session moments.

specificity_gate.description: Self-check before delivery. The post must contain at least 2 of the 3 below:
checks: at least one concrete number (count, days, dollars, percent — must be from the actual session, not invented)
at least one user action verb (asked, tried, removed, picked, broke, fixed, scrapped)
at least one small specific detail unique to this session (a name, a phrase the user said, a tool, a small observation — not generic terms)
specificity_gate.rationale: Posts missing 2+ of these are statistically generic.

length.rule: Most viral BIP single tweets cluster at 180-260 chars across 3-6 short lines. Don't pad to fill 280; don't try to fit everything.

structure.thread_use: rarely — only when the user explicitly asks AND the story genuinely needs 3+ beats

line_breaks.rule: Each line = one beat. Empty lines between beats for breathing room.

hooks.rule: First line stops the scroll. Skip warmups, get to the moment.
avoid_patterns: question_to_audience (unless it's a real question with stake — see question_with_stake template)

emoji.rule: Default to no emoji. Insert only when truly appropriate to the moment. Learn the user's actual emoji selections over time as posts accumulate in .bip-history.json — once 10+ user-edited posts exist, derive a 'preferred' list from actual choices. Until then: zero emoji unless the post genuinely calls for one.

hashtags.rule: Always append the project's branded hashtag on its own trailing line. Auto-detect project name from .bip-config.json (hashtag field) → CLAUDE.md → package.json → cwd basename. Format as #<project-name>, lowercase, preserve hyphens. Exactly one hashtag. Never use generic hashtags.

media.rule: Always suggest media. Video > image. If the user has no recording yet, fall back to screenshot. If no visuals available at all, offer to generate a card image via the bundled script. Be specific about what to capture.
media.video_suggestion_threshold: any working UI demo, before/after change, first run of a feature

personal_vocabulary.$comment: Generic defaults. The user should override these in their own .bip-voice.json with phrases unique to how they actually speak.

remove: API keys / secrets (sk_, pk_, ot_, Bearer, token=)
PII for other people (emails, phone numbers, real names — the post author themselves is fine)
keep: tech stack and tool names (Claude, Supabase, Vercel, etc.)

viral_pattern_stats_reference.$comment: Descriptive stats from a snapshot of 79 viral BIP tweets (500+ likes, 6 months, English). NOT prescriptive — the rules above override when they conflict.

learned_patterns.$comment: Populated by the voice evolution loop. Patterns extracted from user edits to AI-generated posts. Only added after 3+ occurrences and user approval.
